news.headline: CCORF reiterates Buy on Delcath, maintains $21 price target
news.summary: Sell-side coverage stayed constructive with a reiterated Buy and a $21 target, implying meaningful upside from the low-teens share price. The call reflects confidence in the HEPZATO commercial ramp and the widening label-expansion pipeline. Analyst support has held firm through the quiet summer tape.

news.headline: GuruFocus GF Value flags DCTH as undervalued after ~4% pullback
news.summary: A GF Value note argued the stock looks undervalued after a modest drawdown that pushed shares back toward technical support. It frames the weakness as a valuation opportunity rather than fundamental deterioration. Quant/value screens remain supportive.

news.headline: Delcath doses first patient in Phase 2 HEPZATO trial for liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer
news.summary: First patient dosed at the European Institute of Oncology (IEO) in Milan in the global randomized DELUMA study (NCT06875128): HEPZATO plus standard of care vs. SOC alone in HER2-negative mBC, ~90 patients across 20+ US and European sites. This is the flagship catalyst of the period - breast cancer is a far larger market than uveal melanoma, and the indication-expansion strategy is now officially underway in the clinic.

news.headline: Delcath added to Russell 3000, 2500 and Russell 2000 value/microcap indices
news.summary: During the annual FTSE Russell reconstitution, DCTH was added to several Russell value and microcap benchmarks. Index inclusion brings incremental passive-fund ownership and improved institutional visibility, reflecting the higher market cap as HEPZATO revenue scales.

news.headline: Two investigator-initiated HEPZATO melanoma trials-in-progress abstracts at ASCO 2026
news.summary: Two investigator-initiated posters featured at the ASCO Annual Meeting in Chicago: sequential PHP followed by tebentafusp in metastatic uveal melanoma, and PHP plus nivolumab/relatlimab in melanoma with liver metastases. The studies reinforce HEPZATO/PHP as a platform combinable with modern immunotherapy and represent longer-dated pipeline optionality.

news.headline: Q1 2026: revenue $25.0M (+26% YoY), FY guidance reaffirmed at $100M+
news.summary: Q1 revenue rose to $25.0M (from $19.8M) with HEPZATO KIT at $23.3M, ~36% treatment-volume growth, 29 active US centers and 85% gross margin, ending with ~$89M cash and no debt. Management reaffirmed at-least-$100M full-year revenue with positive adjusted EBITDA. A small net loss and a slight revenue miss versus estimates caused near-term pressure, but the growth story stayed intact.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.detail: Second-quarter results expected in early August; the market will watch the HEPZATO revenue trajectory versus the $100M+ full-year guide, new center activations and adjusted EBITDA. Exact date not yet announced.

catalysts.label: HEPZATO center activations
catalysts.detail: 29 active US centers as of Q1 2026; each new commercial account is a direct revenue driver toward the FY guide.

catalysts.label: mCRC Phase 2 expands to 26 centers
catalysts.detail: The Phase 2 colorectal-cancer study is set to grow from 13 to 26 active sites, alongside the newly launched DELUMA breast-cancer trial - broadening the addressable market beyond uveal melanoma.

catalysts.label: mCRC Phase 2 interim data
catalysts.detail: Anticipated interim results from the colorectal-cancer study - potentially the biggest medium-term pipeline catalyst.

pulse: DCTH is in a de-risked commercial-execution phase: HEPZATO revenue grew ~26% YoY in Q1 to $25M on ~36% volume growth, management reaffirmed the $100M+ FY2026 guide with positive adjusted EBITDA, and the balance sheet is clean (~$89M cash, no debt). The last six weeks were quiet but constructive - the flagship item is the July 9 first-patient dosing in the DELUMA breast-cancer Phase 2, a genuine label-expansion catalyst that broadens the story beyond uveal melanoma. Russell index inclusion adds passive demand, and sell-side (Buy, ~$21 target, consensus range roughly $16-31) plus value screens see upside from the low-teens/mid-teens share price after a small pullback toward support. The key near-term test is the early-August Q2 print, where investors want confirmation the revenue ramp and center-activation cadence remain on track. Overall setup is favorable - a near-profitable commercial engine plus multiple free pipeline options - with execution on guidance the main risk.
